faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know about choosing the right LLM.

items.question: Which LLM is best for coding?
items.answer: Claude Sonnet 4.5 is the best for coding with 82% on SWE Bench. It outperforms others on real-world GitHub coding tasks including debugging and code generation.

items.question: Which LLM is best for math and reasoning?
items.answer: For math, Gemini 3 Pro achieves 100% on AIME 2025. For reasoning, Claude 3 Opus leads with 95.4% on GPQA Diamond, the hardest reasoning benchmark.

items.question: How often are rankings updated?
items.answer: Our rankings are updated monthly to reflect the latest model releases and benchmark results from verified providers like vellum.ai.

items.question: Should I always choose the #1 model?
items.answer: Not necessarily. Consider your specific needs: speed (Llama 4 Scout), cost (Nova Micro), or task-specific performance. Use our Quick Picks to find the best match for your use case.

items.question: What benchmarks do you track?
items.answer: We track SWE Bench (coding), AIME 2025 (math), GPQA Diamond (reasoning), ARC-AGI 2 (vision), Humanity's Last Exam (overall), plus cost and speed metrics.

items.question: Are open-source models competitive?
items.answer: Yes! Llama 4 Scout (2600 t/s) and other open models often beat proprietary models on speed and cost while maintaining good quality.
